annotations.label: Sphere packing

anchor.snippet: a sphere packing is an arrangement of non-overlapping spheres within a containing space

annotations.note: No declaration in Mathlib captures the notion of a sphere packing (an arrangement of non-overlapping balls in a space).

annotations.label: Packing density

anchor.snippet: The proportion of space filled by the spheres is called the packing density of the arrangement

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of packing density (proportion of space filled by a packing).

annotations.label: Densest packing in 3D ≈ 74%

anchor.snippet: For equal spheres in three dimensions, the densest packing uses approximately 74% of the volume

annotations.note: No formalization of the optimal 3D sphere packing density (π/√18) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Random packing density ≈ 63.5%

anchor.snippet: A random packing of equal spheres generally has a density around 63.5%

annotations.note: Random close packing density is an empirical / physical statement absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lattice arrangement

anchor.snippet: A lattice arrangement (commonly called a regular arrangement) is one in which the points of the lattice form a very symmetric pattern

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `ZLattice` (a discrete cocompact ℤ-submodule of a real vector space) which underlies lattice packings, but does not formalize lattice packings themselves.

annotations.label: Close-packed structure construction

anchor.snippet: the densest packing of equal spheres is achieved by a family of structures called close-packed structures

annotations.note: Close-packed (FCC/HCP) structures are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cubic and hexagonal close packing

anchor.snippet: Two simple arrangements within the close-packed family correspond to regular arrangements

annotations.note: FCC and HCP lattices are not constructed or named in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kepler conjecture (Hales)

anchor.snippet: Johannes Kepler conjectured that this is the maximum possible density amongst both regular and irregular arrangements

annotations.note: The Kepler conjecture is not in Mathlib (Hales's Flyspeck proof lives in HOL Light).

annotations.label: Gauss: densest lattice packing

anchor.snippet: Carl Friedrich Gauss proved in 1831 that these packings have the highest density amongst all possible lattice packings

annotations.note: Gauss's optimality of FCC among 3D lattice packings is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Other common lattice packing densities

anchor.snippet: These include the cubic lattice with a density of

annotations.note: Mathlib does not compute packing densities for common lattices.

annotations.label: Rigid/jammed packing

anchor.section: Jammed packings with a low density
anchor.snippet: Packings where all spheres are constrained by their neighbours to stay in one location are called rigid or jammed

annotations.note: The notion of a jammed/rigid packing has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Lowest-density strictly jammed regular packing

anchor.section: Jammed packings with a low density
anchor.snippet: regular sphere packing with the lowest known density is a diluted

annotations.note: No statement about lowest-density jammed packings appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Random close packing density

anchor.snippet: This irregular packing will generally have a density of about 64%

annotations.note: Random close packing density is empirical and not represented in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Densest lattice packings known dimensions

anchor.snippet: The densest lattice packings of hyperspheres are known for 1–8 and 24 dimensions

annotations.note: The list of dimensions where the densest lattice packing is known is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Viazovska: E8 optimal in 8D

anchor.snippet: Maryna Viazovska announced a proof that the E 8 lattice

annotations.note: Viazovska's E8 sphere-packing theorem is not in Mathlib; the E8 lattice itself is also not defined.

annotations.label: Leech lattice optimal in 24D

anchor.snippet: she and a group of collaborators announced a similar proof that the Leech lattice

annotations.note: Cohn–Kumar–Miller–Radchenko–Viazovska's Leech lattice result is not formalized in Mathlib; the Leech lattice is not defined.

annotations.label: Asymptotic bounds on densest lattice

anchor.snippet: It is known that for large n , the densest lattice in dimension n has density

annotations.note: Asymptotic Minkowski–Hlawka / Kabatyanskii–Levenshtein bounds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Campos–Jenssen–Michelen–Sahasrabudhe lower bound

anchor.snippet: Marcelo Campos, Matthew Jenssen, Marcus Michelen and Julian Sahasrabudhe announced an improvement to the lower bound

annotations.note: This 2023 lower-bound improvement is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Klartag improved lower bound

anchor.snippet: Bo'az Klartag announced a significant further improvement

annotations.note: Klartag's further lower-bound improvement is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Small interstitial sphere bound

anchor.snippet: it is always possible to insert some smaller spheres of up to 0.290 99 of the radius of the larger sphere

annotations.note: Bounds on interstitial sphere radii are not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Octahedral hole radius threshold

anchor.snippet: When the smaller sphere has a radius greater than 0.414 21 of the radius of the larger sphere, it is no longer possible to fit into even the octahedral holes

annotations.note: The octahedral-hole radius bound √2−1 is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Structures exceeding close packing density

anchor.snippet: Structures are known that exceed the close packing density for radius ratios up to 0.659 786

annotations.note: Unequal-radius packings exceeding close packing density are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bounds for strictly jammed packings with any radii

anchor.snippet: The upper bound for the density of a strictly jammed sphere packing with any set of radii is 1

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks bounds on jammed unequal-sphere packings.

annotations.label: Böröczky upper bound in hyperbolic space

anchor.snippet: K. Böröczky gives a universal upper bound for the density of sphere packings of hyperbolic

annotations.note: Böröczky's hyperbolic packing bound is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Böröczky bound in 3D hyperbolic space

anchor.snippet: In three dimensions the Böröczky bound is approximately 85.327 613 %

annotations.note: The 3D hyperbolic Böröczky constant is not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contact graph of a packing

anchor.snippet: The contact graph of an arbitrary finite packing of unit balls is the graph whose vertices correspond to the packing elements

annotations.note: Contact graphs of sphere packings are not defined in Mathlib (though `SimpleGraph` is).

annotations.label: Bezdek–Reid upper bounds on touching tuples

anchor.snippet: non-trivial upper bounds on the number of touching pairs, triplets, and quadruples

annotations.note: Bezdek–Reid contact-number bounds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sticky-sphere problem

anchor.snippet: The problem of finding the arrangement of n identical spheres that maximizes the number of contact points between the spheres is known as the "sticky-sphere problem"

annotations.note: The sticky-sphere optimization problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sphere packing and error-correcting codes

anchor.snippet: Sphere packing on the corners of a hypercube

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory linking sphere packings to error-correcting codes (no Hamming/linear-code packing infrastructure found).
